largar.reason: The 'cuidado' claims rioplatense Spanish uses 'largar' in a more limited way than Portuguese. This is doubtful: rioplatense Spanish colloquially uses 'largar' very broadly and similarly to the Portuguese senses given here — 'largar el laburo' (quit the job), 'largar a la novia/al novio' (dump the partner), 'largá eso' (drop/let go of something) are all common. The claimed contrast with Portuguese usage is not verifiable and likely overstated/false.
